name: African Wild Dog
description: Perseverance, teamwork, and stamina. We're not describing a sports team, but this unique African canine.
understandResult: The African Wild Dog has come up with the perfect hunting strategy for each animal, leading to a high rate of success. You are organized, and come up with an attack plan before you pursue a goal.
The most sociable dogs in the world, their packs of 10-30 dogs have even been observed behavior akin to voting! You make decisions based on how other people will feel, and your values guide you.
If a large enough predator asserts itself, they might need to back down from a catch. You need time to recharge, and can get overwhelmed in large groups.
African Wild Dogs require large swats of territory to maintain their packs. You enjoy exploring new ideas and possibilities.

As individuals, they must be cautious as they can be picked off by other predators. But as a unit, African Wild Dogs are confident and powerful. You are resilient and confident amongst friends, but may struggle with confidence from time to time.

facts.Physical Description: Their fur is red, black, white, brown and yellow in a random pattern, leading them to be called the painted dog. Four toes are on each paw, and they have round, perky ears.
facts.Predator or Prey?: African Wild Dogs hunt in a pack, and their targets are usually much faster and larger. As a result, they are known for their "dogged" persistence in chasing after their prey until it is too tired to continue. Then, they corner the animal as a pack. Wild Dogs have a distinct hunting strategy for each type of prey, leading to a success rate between 60-90%. Examples of those prey consist of antelope, warthog, and wildebeest.
facts.Community and Trust: The most sociable dogs in the world, their packs have strict ranking systems, led by the dominant breeding pair. Within the pack, they raise their young, care for their sick, and eat together. Packs are composed of 10-30 individuals. Some scientists have even observed behavior akin to "voting," through the use of sneezes when the pack rallies!
facts.Territorial or Shy?: These dogs must coexist with a wide variety of other predators and dangerous prey. Their small size relative to other predators requires them to abandon some prey if a large enough competitor asserts itself. Adaptability in situations with others is an important part of their survival.
facts.Does it Migrate?: Since African Wild Dogs maintain some territory but require large expanses to maintain their packs, they roam large areas of their Savannah homes.
facts.Food Chain: As individuals, they must be cautious as they can be picked off by other predators. But as a unit, African Wild Dogs are confident and powerful.
facts.Endangered Status: Habitat destruction and human intervention have led the African Wild Dog to critically endangered status by the IUCN Red List for all of its subpopulations.
